tasks.title: Reduce Chromium V8 memory cap from 2048 to 100 MB
tasks.description: In lib/sharedBrowser.ts, change '--js-flags=--max-old-space-size=2048' to '--js-flags=--max-old-space-size=100'. The 2048 MB flag is appropriate for desktop but wastes RAM on 512 MB hosts.
acceptance_criteria: CHROMIUM_LAUNCH_ARGS contains --js-flags=--max-old-space-size=100
tasks.test_strategy: Unit: verify CHROMIUM_LAUNCH_ARGS array contains correct flag. Integration: launch Chromium, verify no V8 OOM on ppomppu board page.

tasks.title: Fix systemd ExecStart to Node.js with memory flags (was: npm run dev)
tasks.description: DONE IN THIS SESSION. ops/systemd/marketing-automation.service was running 'npm run dev' (Vite HMR + tsx) in production — a critical bug. Fixed to: node --max-old-space-size=400 --expose-gc --enable-source-maps tsx server.ts with NODE_ENV=production, MemoryHigh=450M, MemoryMax=500M, LimitNOFILE=65536, KillMode=mixed. Also adds --no-sandbox to CHROMIUM_LAUNCH_ARGS if required by the cloud host's seccomp profile.
acceptance_criteria: systemd ExecStart uses node directly with --max-old-space-size=400 --expose-gc
NODE_ENV=production set
MemoryMax=500M in [Service]
tasks.test_strategy: systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl restart marketing-automation && curl localhost:3000/api/health

tasks.title: Verify Chromium launch args and fix storage state path
tasks.description: DONE IN THIS SESSION. Confirmed --disable-dev-shm-usage present. Also fixed STORAGE_STATE_PATH from ephemeral 'artifacts/browser-storage-state.json' (relative to CWD) to path.join(ENV.BOT_PROFILE_DIR, 'browser-storage-state.json') (persistent volume). Also reduced --js-flags from 2048 to 100 MB (T-001). If cloud host has seccomp restrictions, add --no-sandbox to CHROMIUM_LAUNCH_ARGS.
acceptance_criteria: --disable-dev-shm-usage present
STORAGE_STATE_PATH resolves to BOT_PROFILE_DIR
--js-flags capped at 100 MB
tasks.test_strategy: grep for flags in lib/sharedBrowser.ts; verify STORAGE_STATE_PATH uses path.join(ENV.BOT_PROFILE_DIR, ...)

tasks.title: Create .dockerignore file
tasks.description: Create .dockerignore at repo root excluding: node_modules, .env, .env.*, artifacts/, *.log, *.json.bak, ppomppu_profile/, dist/, .git, .venv, tests/, .claude/, .planning/. This prevents sensitive files and build artifacts from entering Docker layers.
acceptance_criteria: .dockerignore exists with specified exclusions
tasks.test_strategy: Build Docker image, verify excluded paths not present in layers.

tasks.title: Add systemd memory limit to service file
tasks.description: DONE IN THIS SESSION as part of T-002 fix. MemoryHigh=450M and MemoryMax=500M added. Also added LimitNOFILE=65536 (Playwright needs many FDs) and KillMode=mixed (SIGTERM forwarding). Verify cgroup limits with: systemctl show marketing-automation --property=MemoryMax
acceptance_criteria: systemd service file includes MemoryMax=500M and MemoryHigh=450M
LimitNOFILE=65536 present
tasks.test_strategy: systemctl show marketing-automation --property=MemoryMax — should show 524288000

tasks.title: Run 2-hour memory stability test
tasks.description: With T-001 through T-005 applied, run the bot in DRY_RUN_MODE=true for 2 hours. Monitor RSS via /api/health/resources every 15 minutes. Verify RSS stays under 400 MB and is stable (no progressive growth > 50 MB over the period).
acceptance_criteria: RSS stable under 400 MB for 2 hours
No OOM kills in dmesg
Chromium launches on every scheduler tick
tasks.test_strategy: Manual: Set DRY_RUN_MODE=true, start service, poll /api/health/resources every 15 min, record RSS values.

tasks.title: Create multi-stage Dockerfile
tasks.description: Create Dockerfile at repo root with 3 stages: (1) frontend-build: node:20-alpine, npm ci, npm run build → dist/. (2) server-deps: node:20-alpine, npm ci --omit=dev, npx playwright install chromium --with-deps. (3) runtime: node:20-alpine, install ca-certificates + dumb-init, create app user, COPY from previous stages, ENTRYPOINT with dumb-init, CMD with node --max-old-space-size=400 --expose-gc tsx server.ts.
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile builds successfully
Final image size < 800 MB
Container starts and /api/health returns 200
tasks.test_strategy: Build: docker build -t marketing-automation:latest . Check size: docker images. Run: docker run -p 3000:3000 marketing-automation:latest. Verify: curl localhost:3000/api/health.

tasks.title: Create docker-compose.yml with shm_size and volumes
tasks.description: Create docker-compose.yml with: services.app.build from Dockerfile, shm_size: 256mb, volumes for shared data (activity_log, bot_profile), ports 3000:3000, env_file or environment for required vars, healthcheck on /api/health. Add named volumes for persistent data.
acceptance_criteria: docker compose up -d starts container
shm_size: 256mb configured
Persistent volumes mounted
tasks.test_strategy: docker compose up -d, docker compose ps (verify running), docker compose logs (verify startup), curl localhost:3000/api/health.

tasks.title: Add non-root USER in Docker runtime stage
tasks.description: Ensure Dockerfile runtime stage creates an 'app' user (addgroup -S app && adduser -S app -G app) and switches to it with USER app before CMD. This follows least-privilege principle.
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile contains USER app before CMD
Container runs as non-root user
tasks.test_strategy: docker run --rm marketing-automation:latest whoami — should output 'app'.

tasks.title: Add dumb-init for PID 1 signal forwarding
tasks.description: Install dumb-init in Dockerfile runtime stage (apk add --no-cache dumb-init). Set ENTRYPOINT to ["dumb-init", "--"]. This ensures SIGTERM from docker stop reaches Node.js, triggering graceful shutdown via registerSignalHandlers().
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile ENTRYPOINT uses dumb-init
docker stop gracefully shuts down (no force kill)
tasks.test_strategy: docker run -d --name test marketing-automation:latest, docker stop test, verify logs show graceful shutdown.

tasks.title: Verify Docker image size under 800 MB
tasks.description: Build Docker image and verify final size is under 800 MB. If over, investigate: use node:20-slim instead of alpine if Playwright deps are too large on Alpine, or exclude unnecessary system packages.
acceptance_criteria: docker images shows marketing-automation:latest < 800 MB
tasks.test_strategy: docker build -t marketing-automation:latest . && docker images marketing-automation.

tasks.title: Smoke test Docker container end-to-end
tasks.description: Run docker compose up -d with DRY_RUN_MODE=true. Verify: (1) container starts, (2) /api/health returns 200, (3) Chromium launches (check logs for shared_browser.started), (4) scheduler ticks (check logs for scheduler.tick).
acceptance_criteria: Container starts and stays running
/api/health returns 200
Chromium launches
Scheduler ticks logged
tasks.test_strategy: docker compose up -d, wait 30s, curl localhost:3000/api/health, docker compose logs | grep shared_browser.started.

tasks.title: Add release pruning to deploy-release.sh
tasks.description: After the symlink swap in ops/deploy/deploy-release.sh, add pruning: ls -dt "$RELEASES_DIR"/*/ | tail -n +6 | xargs rm -rf. This keeps only the 5 most recent releases and prevents disk exhaustion.
acceptance_criteria: After 6th deploy, oldest release directory is deleted
At most 5 release directories exist
tasks.test_strategy: Simulate 6 deploys to a test directory, verify only 5 remain.

tasks.title: Verify persistent volume paths for ACTIVITY_LOG_PATH and BOT_PROFILE_DIR
tasks.description: Review config/env.ts defaults and docker-compose.yml volume mounts to ensure ACTIVITY_LOG_PATH and BOT_PROFILE_DIR resolve to /opt/marketing-automation/shared/ (or Docker named volumes), not container-internal ephemeral paths.
acceptance_criteria: ACTIVITY_LOG_PATH defaults to or is overridden to persistent path
BOT_PROFILE_DIR defaults to or is overridden to persistent path
Docker volumes mount these paths to host or named volume
tasks.test_strategy: Check env defaults in config/env.ts. In Docker, verify volume mounts in docker-compose.yml map to persistent storage.

tasks.title: Add pre-deploy dry-run migration check
tasks.description: Add a pre-deploy step in deploy-release.sh that runs NODE_ENV=production tsx scripts/migrate.ts --dry-run (create this script if it doesn't exist). If migrations are detected, warn the operator and offer to take a SQLite snapshot first.
acceptance_criteria: Deploy script runs migration dry-run before proceeding
Warnings displayed if schema changes detected
tasks.test_strategy: Create scripts/migrate.ts with --dry-run flag that checks for pending migrations. Test deploy with and without pending migrations.

tasks.title: Verify rollback health check exit behavior
tasks.description: ALREADY IMPLEMENTED: rollback-release.sh line 20 already calls healthcheck.sh, which exits 1 on timeout. Verification task only: (1) confirm healthcheck.sh times out correctly with HEALTH_MAX_WAIT_SECONDS, (2) confirm rollback exits non-zero on health failure (set -euo pipefail propagates the healthcheck.sh exit code), (3) test: stop service port, run rollback, verify exit code is non-zero.
acceptance_criteria: rollback-release.sh exits non-zero if healthcheck fails
HEALTH_MAX_WAIT_SECONDS is configurable (already implemented)
tasks.test_strategy: PORT=9999 bash ops/deploy/rollback-release.sh (no service on 9999) — expect exit code 1

tasks.title: Document deploy and rollback runbook
tasks.description: Create .planning/cloud-deployment-optimization/deploy-runbook.md documenting: (1) How to build a release tarball, (2) How to deploy, (3) How to rollback, (4) How to set up UptimeRobot monitoring, (5) Troubleshooting common issues (OOM, Chromium crashes, SQLite locks).
acceptance_criteria: Runbook covers build, deploy, rollback, monitoring, troubleshooting
tasks.test_strategy: Peer review — another person follows runbook steps and succeeds without additional guidance.

tasks.title: Harden competitor-ad-sqlite.ts with full PRAGMA set
tasks.description: ARCHITECTURE CORRECTION: kakaoDb.ts uses PostgreSQL (pg.Pool), NOT SQLite. The only better-sqlite3 DB is in lib/competitor-ad-sqlite.ts (openDatabase()). It already has journal_mode=WAL. Add the remaining NAS-hardening PRAGMAs: synchronous=NORMAL, cache_size=-16000, temp_store=MEMORY, mmap_size=134217728, busy_timeout=5000. Target: lib/competitor-ad-sqlite.ts:openDatabase(), lines 53-55.
acceptance_criteria: openDatabase() applies all 6 PRAGMAs after new Database()
journal_mode=WAL already present — add the remaining 5
tasks.test_strategy: npx tsx -e "import { openDatabase } from './lib/competitor-ad-sqlite.js'; const db = openDatabase(); console.log(db.pragma('journal_mode')[0]);"

tasks.title: Review and tune activity log GC constants
tasks.description: CONSTANT CHANGE ONLY — NOT new code. rotateActivityLog() is FULLY IMPLEMENTED in lib/resourceMonitor.ts. Current constants: MAX_ACTIVITY_LOG_ENTRIES=1000, KEEP_ACTIVITY_LOG_ENTRIES=500. The spec says 5000, but at current log size (1.4 MB / ~1000 entries) that is ~7 MB — still acceptable for a 512 MB host. Decision: keep at 1000/500 (more conservative, better for NAS I/O) OR raise to 5000/4500 if analytics coverage is needed. Update FR-008 cap target to match whichever is chosen. Also verify RSS_WARN_MB was lowered from 1024 to 400 (DONE in this session).
acceptance_criteria: MAX_ACTIVITY_LOG_ENTRIES value is consciously chosen and matches FR-008
rotateActivityLog() called by runGarbageCollection() (already wired)
tasks.test_strategy: grep for MAX_ACTIVITY_LOG_ENTRIES in lib/resourceMonitor.ts; run GC with oversize log and verify truncation.

tasks.title: Verify activity log read latency under 100 ms
tasks.description: With 5000 entries in activity_log.json, measure read time of GET /api/logs. Should be under 100 ms. If over, consider: (a) lazy loading with pagination, (b) migration to SQLite table.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/logs with 5000 entries completes in < 100 ms
tasks.test_strategy: Generate 5000 synthetic log entries, time curl -w '%{time_total}' localhost:3000/api/logs.

tasks.title: Add periodic GC (6h interval) to server.ts — production only
tasks.description: DONE IN THIS SESSION. setInterval with 6-hour cadence added to startServer() in server.ts. Guards: (1) only when NODE_ENV=production, (2) only when !skipBot. Uses .unref() so the timer doesn't block clean process exit. Logs resource.gc_periodic event with artifact/log counts.
acceptance_criteria: setInterval registered in startServer() under NODE_ENV=production guard
Timer uses .unref()
Log event resource.gc_periodic emitted on each GC run
tasks.test_strategy: NODE_ENV=production grep in server.ts for gc_periodic; integration test: mock timers or check log output.

tasks.title: Verify /api/health/resources response completeness
tasks.description: VERIFY ONLY — already fully implemented. GET /api/health/resources calls getResourceMetrics() + checkResourceThresholds() and spreads the full ResourceMetrics object (routes/api/health.ts:28-32). Fields available: process.rssMb, process.heapUsedMb, process.heapTotalMb, activityLog.fileSizeKb, activityLog.entryCount, activityLog.exceedsMax, artifacts.totalSizeMb, warnings[]. Acceptance: curl the endpoint and confirm all fields present. If any alias is needed for external dashboards, add it.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/health/resources returns process.rssMb, process.heapUsedMb, activityLog.entryCount, artifacts.totalSizeMb, warnings[]
tasks.test_strategy: NODE_ENV=development DEV_SKIP_BOT=true npm run dev & sleep 3 && curl -s localhost:3000/api/health/resources | jq .process

tasks.title: Add express.static cache headers for production
tasks.description: In server.ts where express.static serves dist/, add { maxAge: '7d', etag: true } options. Vite content-hashes filenames so long cache is safe. This reduces repeated asset fetches on dashboard reloads.
acceptance_criteria: express.static configured with maxAge: '7d' and etag: true
Response headers include Cache-Control: public, max-age=604800
tasks.test_strategy: curl -I localhost:3000/assets/main-<hash>.js, verify Cache-Control header.

tasks.title: Document UptimeRobot + Slack alert setup
tasks.description: Add to deploy-runbook.md: (1) Create UptimeRobot account, (2) Add HTTP monitor for https://<domain>/api/health, check every 5 min, (3) Configure Slack webhook alert to #alerts channel, (4) Set up alert escalation rules.
acceptance_criteria: Runbook includes UptimeRobot setup steps with screenshots or URLs
tasks.test_strategy: Follow runbook steps to set up monitoring on a live deployment.

tasks.title: Define NL router tool schema
tasks.description: Create tool/function schema for NL webhook router: get_status, set_gap_threshold (threshold: int 1-20), trigger_observer, trigger_publish, set_dry_run (enabled: bool), pause_scheduler, resume_scheduler. Save as lib/nl-tools-schema.json.
acceptance_criteria: Schema file exists with all 7 tools defined
Each tool has name, description, parameters with types and constraints
tasks.test_strategy: Validate schema against OpenAI function calling spec format.

tasks.title: Implement Gemini Flash function calling in NL router
tasks.description: In routes/api/nl.ts, add Gemini Flash integration: parse incoming Slack message → forward to Gemini with tool schema → execute returned tool call → post result back to Slack. Use openai SDK compatible endpoint. Add kill-switch via env var NL_WEBHOOK_LLM_ENABLED.
acceptance_criteria: Natural language message routes to correct tool call
Result posted back to Slack
Kill-switch works (NL_WEBHOOK_LLM_ENABLED=false disables LLM)
tasks.test_strategy: Send test messages via webhook, verify correct tool calls executed. Test with malformed input, verify graceful error handling.

tasks.title: Add HMAC verification for Slack webhook [SECURITY - P1]
tasks.description: SECURITY GATE — must be merged before T-026 is deployed. The NL webhook can trigger_publish, set_dry_run=false, and pause/resume the scheduler — it is a remote code execution surface. Verify Slack's X-Slack-Signature header (HMAC-SHA256 of 'v0:{timestamp}:{body}' with NL_WEBHOOK_SECRET as key). Reject requests older than 5 minutes (replay protection). Reference: tests/integration/nlWebhook.test.ts for existing signature logic. Slack uses v0: prefix scheme, not the current custom scheme.
acceptance_criteria: Requests without valid X-Slack-Signature are rejected with 401
Requests older than 5 minutes are rejected with 401 (replay protection)
Valid signed requests pass through
Tests in nlWebhook.test.ts pass
tasks.test_strategy: npm run test:integration — existing nlWebhook tests must pass. Add Slack v0: signature format test.

tasks.title: Add rate limiting to NL endpoint (5 req/min)
tasks.description: Apply a stricter rate limit (5 req/min) to the NL webhook endpoint. This is stricter than the default limiter to prevent abuse of LLM API calls.
acceptance_criteria: NL endpoint returns 429 after 5 requests per minute
Other endpoints unaffected
tasks.test_strategy: Send 6 rapid requests to NL endpoint, verify 6th returns 429.

tasks.title: End-to-end NL webhook test with Slack
tasks.description: Deploy to a publicly accessible endpoint (ngrok or live VM), configure Slack app webhook URL, send natural language commands, verify bot responds correctly. Test: 'what's the status?', 'set gap to 5', 'pause the bot', 'trigger a publish run'.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 test commands work correctly
HMAC verification passes for Slack requests
Rate limiting works under real Slack traffic
tasks.test_strategy: Live test with real Slack workspace and deployed bot.

task_summary.total_effort_estimate: ~8-12 hours of active work + 2 hours wall-clock for stress test
